content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes three numbers as input and prints "**Increasing**" if the numbers are in strictly increasing order, "**Decreasing**" if they are in strictly decreasing order, and "**Neither**" otherwise.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers a, b and c.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings: "Increasing" if a<b<c, "Decreasing" if a>b>c, otherwise "Neither".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 20 < 30 < 41 holds, so the sequence is strictly increasing.
50 > 30 > 20 holds, so the sequence is strictly decreasing.
The third number equals the second, breaking strict monotonicity; therefore the sequence is neither increasing nor decreasing.

solution.approach: For each test case read the three integers. Compare them using strict inequalities. If a<b and b<c, the sequence is strictly increasing. Else if a>b and b>c, it is strictly decreasing. Otherwise it is neither. Output the corresponding word. This runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
